trim_reasons: Spread of -22.5 is the largest blowout risk on the slate — even with the elite playmaker exemption, SGA routinely sits in garbage time of dominant OKC wins, and a min_floor of 28.7 means a legitimate early exit is within range. The blowout penalty was applied but a 22.5-point spread is extreme enough that Q4 rest risk is not fully priced by a single percentage adjustment — AST is the stat most sensitive to reduced late-game touches.

VOLATILE tag with a BLOWOUT_RISK spread of -9.5 means Tatum could be pulled in the fourth quarter of a decided game; FG_MARGIN_NEG already forced a step-down, indicating a shaky recent shooting floor, and even at T15 the volatile distribution means a cold-shooting early exit is a realistic miss scenario.

opp_defense is rated tough for 3PM, and the BLOWOUT_RISK game script means Tatum's volume could compress if BOS goes up big and rotates to bench scorers early; 3PM is the most volume-dependent prop and a lopsided game script directly threatens attempt count even for a 10/10 recent hitter.

min_floor of 22.7 is below the 24-minute structural floor threshold, meaning a single normal-variance low-minutes game produces a miss; FG_COLD at -13% signals a recent shooting slump that, combined with the minutes fragility, creates a genuine floor risk even at the conservative T10 tier.

MIN is rated tough for 3PM defense, and LaMelo's min_floor of 22.7 means a minutes-restricted game could suppress attempt volume below the threshold needed to reliably clear 2 threes; the combination of tough 3PM defense and fragile minutes floor is a credible bear case.

min_floor of 21.0 is materially below the 24-minute safety threshold, and FG_COLD at exactly -15% (the mandatory step-down trigger) signals genuine recent shooting suppression — a younger role player on a road game against MIN with sub-24 minute floor protection is one early foul or lineup change away from a miss.
